title: phegopteris zayuensis
intro:
Phegopteris zayuensis is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae, commonly known as a type of wild fern native to the Sichuan and Yunnan provinces of China.

This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for treating fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Phegopteris zayuensis include its ability to reduce inflammation, relieve coughs and colds, and act as an expectorant to help clear mucus from the lungs. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often prepared into teas, infusions, or dried extracts for its medicinal uses, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and potential side effects.
